title: Rhynchosinapis johnstonii
taxonomy: Rhynchosinapis johnstonii is classified as a species within the genus Rhynchosinapis, which in turn is part of the Brassicaceae family. This family is commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. The Brassicaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Rhynchosinapis johnstonii is a unique species within this family, but its exact classification within the family is not well-documented. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification of Rhynchosinapis johnstonii.
intro:
I couldn't find any information on Rhynchosinapis johnstonii. However, I did find information on Rhynchosinapis. Rhynchosinapis is a genus of flowering plants in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard family. 

Rhynchosinapis arvensis, also known as wild mustard, is a plant that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. It has been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits in treating various conditions such as wounds, gastrointestinal issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Rhynchosinapis arvensis include isothiocyanates, glucosinolates, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rhynchosinapis arvensis include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support digestive health.
